the sim opens on Spectral Schematics, which the field rarely builds (0.4% pick) — a sim-only call; compare the meta cores below

coachLine: Sim-optimal for skirmish uptime: first spike lands around minute 12 (Spectral Schematics), third item by minute 25. Take Demiurge: +18.8% on 20-second fights at minute 15.

metaBuilds.title: AP Frontline
metaBuilds.whyLine: Strongest meta core for physical survivability, online around minute 25 — about 4,295 effective HP vs physical in the sim.
metaBuilds.optimizer: Optimizer agrees: within 2% of our best core on physical survivability. The winrate is earned, not luck.

metaBuilds.title: AP Frontline
metaBuilds.whyLine: Strongest meta core for physical survivability, online around minute 25 — about 4,206 effective HP vs physical in the sim.
metaBuilds.optimizer: Optimizer agrees: within 4% of our best core on physical survivability. The winrate is earned, not luck.

metaBuilds.title: AP Burst
metaBuilds.whyLine: Strongest meta core for one-combo burst, online around minute 25 — about 1,904 damage in one combo in the sim.
metaBuilds.optimizer: Optimizer agrees: within 0% of our best core on one-combo burst. The winrate is earned, not luck.

matchups.gameplan: You hold the kill-threat edge wherever one exists: play front-foot, but respect jungle attention. Renna's main threat is Mortal Coil; bait it before you commit, and mixed resist softens the trade.

counterSwap.line: Losing lane? Third item Void Helm instead of Spectral Schematics: their 3s all-in loses 25% of its bite; you give up 26% of your damage.

matchups.gameplan: You hold the kill-threat edge wherever one exists: play front-foot, but respect jungle attention. Iggy & Scorch deals area damage — spread out so one cast can't catch the group.

counterSwap.line: Losing lane? Third item Void Helm instead of Spectral Schematics: their 3s all-in loses 26% of its bite; you give up 26% of your damage.

matchups.gameplan: You hold the kill-threat edge wherever one exists: play front-foot, but respect jungle attention. Lt. Belica executes low targets with Neural Disruptor — don't linger below half HP.

counterSwap.line: Losing lane? Third item Void Helm instead of Spectral Schematics: their 3s all-in loses 23% of its bite; you give up 28% of your damage.

matchups.gameplan: Survive the early checkpoints, concede even trades, and take over once your spike lands. Gideon deals area damage — spread out so one cast can't catch the group.

counterSwap.line: Losing lane? Third item Void Helm instead of Spectral Schematics: their 3s all-in loses 24% of its bite; you give up 25% of your damage.

matchups.gameplan: You hold the kill-threat edge wherever one exists: play front-foot, but respect jungle attention. Countess deals area damage — spread out so one cast can't catch the group.

counterSwap.line: Losing lane? Third item Void Helm instead of Spectral Schematics: their 3s all-in loses 20% of its bite; you give up 28% of your damage.

matchups.gameplan: You hold the kill-threat edge wherever one exists: play front-foot, but respect jungle attention. Argus deals area damage — spread out so one cast can't catch the group.

counterSwap.line: Losing lane? Third item Void Helm instead of Spectral Schematics: their 3s all-in loses 25% of its bite; you give up 25% of your damage.

laneSteer.provenance: “Wild Growth” ⇒ poke; the sim can’t model this augment — steered by the declared playstyle + field evidence — field midlane 51.1% over 463,451 games, magnitude not simulated.

matchups.gameplan: You hold the kill-threat edge wherever one exists: play front-foot, but respect jungle attention. Zinx's main threat is Ricochet; bait it before you commit, and mixed resist softens the trade.
You hold the kill-threat edge wherever one exists: play front-foot, but respect jungle attention. Adele deals area damage — spread out so one cast can't catch the group.

counterSwap.line: Losing lane? Third item Warden's Faith instead of Unbroken Will: their 3s all-in loses 27% of its bite; you give up 0% of your damage.

matchups.gameplan: You hold the kill-threat edge wherever one exists: play front-foot, but respect jungle attention. Riktor ramps on auto-attacks — pressure them before they itemize, and mixed resist blunts them later.
You hold the kill-threat edge wherever one exists: play front-foot, but respect jungle attention. Dekker deals area damage — spread out so one cast can't catch the group.
You hold the kill-threat edge wherever one exists: play front-foot, but respect jungle attention. Phase's main threat is Energy Lance; bait it before you commit, and mixed resist softens the trade.
